job_title: Senior Analyst - American Football, Data and Insights (D&I)
job_nlp_title: Senior Analyst - American Football, Data and Insights (D&I)
taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Data Analysis, Data Scientist
core_skills: Data Science, Quantitative Analysis, SQL, Python, R
skill_match_scores: Data Science:1.0, Quantitative Analysis:1.0, SQL:1.0, Python:1.0, R:0.0
must_have_quals: A degree in a quantitative discipline (Analytics, Data Science, Business, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics), 3+ years of experience in an analytics role performing quantitative analysis with big data to help guide decisions, 1+ years of experience independently managing projects and building analysis plans with clear goals & timelines, Expertise in SQL and ability to extract data from large data sources with differing structures, Proficiency analyzing large datasets in programmatic analysis tools (e.g. Python, R), Experience with data visualization software (e.g. Tableau, PowerBI, Looker), Experience in statistical modeling (e.g. regression, clustering, predictive modeling), Passion for playing and thinking about games
core_responsibilities: Report to the Director of Sports Analytics for the Tiburon Studio, Collaborate with the Live Service team to establish key metrics and targets to measure program and content performance against expectations, Receive questions from our partners, determine how our data can help answer them, and provide insights through reports, presentations, or dashboards, Lead presentations to diverse audiences including senior management, Help to shape and implement analytics best practices and team vision
job_summary: Electronic Arts (EA) creates next-level entertainment experiences that inspire players and fans around the world. The Senior Analyst will be part of the Data & Insights team, leading critical insights into the operations, processes, and tools that drive the creation of American Football games.
